content.problemStatement:
Given a list of NNN integers, representing height of mountains. Find the height of the tallest mountain.

Input:
First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
The first line in each testcase contains one integer, NNN.
The following line contains NNN space separated integers: the height of each mountains.
Output:For each testcase, output one line with one integer: the height of the tallest mountain for that test case.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the number of mountains; the second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the heights of the mountains.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing one integer – the maximum height among the given mountains.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The heights are [4,7,6,3,1]; the largest value is 7.
Only one mountain exists with height 10^9, so that is the answer.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simply scanning through the array of mountain heights and keeping track of the maximum height encountered so far. This approach works because the maximum height is the only value of interest, and it can be found in a single pass through the data.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input, as only a constant amount of space is used to store the maximum height and other variables.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());long maxHeight=Long.MIN_VALUE;for(int i=0;i<N;i++){ long h=Long.parseLong(st.nextToken()); if(h>maxHeight) maxHeight=h; } sb.append(maxHeight).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
